title: Count Valid Triangles in Array
description:
Given an array of nonnegative integers representing side lengths, count how many triplets can form a triangle. A triangle is valid if a + b > c for sorted sides a ≤ b ≤ c.

    

Constraints:
    - 0 ≤ n ≤ 1000
- 0 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10^3
explanation:
Intuition
Sort the array. Fix the largest side c and use a two-pointer sweep on the left to count pairs (a,b) with a+b>c. When a+b>c holds, all pairs between a..b are valid; move b left. Otherwise, move a right. This gives quadratic time.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Sort nums ascending
2. Main Algorithm: For k from n-1 down to 2, set i=0, j=k-1 and count pairs with a+b>nums[k], updating the total
3. Return Result: Return total count

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n^2)
- Space Complexity: O(1)

Key Insights
- Monotonicity lets you count ranges at once
- Zeros cannot form a positive triangle

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to sort breaks the two-pointer logic
- Using ≥ instead of > will overcount degenerate triangles
hints: Fix largest and two-pointer the rest
